name: grpcurl (cURL for gRPC)

auth.notes: TLS, mTLS, custom headers for auth tokens

performance.notes: CLI tool; single request at a time (not load testing)

agent_readiness.notes: cURL equivalent for gRPC services. grpcurl -plaintext localhost:50051 list (list services). grpcurl -plaintext localhost:50051 describe (describe schema). grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"name":"world"}' localhost:50051 helloworld.Greeter/SayHello. Requires server reflection or .proto files (-proto flag). TLS: remove -plaintext. mTLS: --cert/--key. Streaming supported. -H for headers. JSON input/output. Essential tool for gRPC API testing and debugging.

gotchas: Server reflection must be enabled or .proto files needed
Protobuf field names differ from JSON (camelCase vs snake_case)
Complex message types require careful JSON construction
best_when: Ad-hoc gRPC endpoint testing and debugging
Listing and describing gRPC service methods
Shell scripting gRPC calls in testing pipelines
avoid_when: GUI-driven API exploration (Postman/Insomnia have gRPC support)
summary: grpcurl is the cURL for gRPC — command-line gRPC client. grpcurl -plaintext localhost:50051 list to discover services. grpcurl -d '{"field":"value"}' host:port package.Service/Method to invoke. Requires server reflection or .proto files. JSON input/output. Streaming support. TLS and mTLS. Essential tool for gRPC development and debugging.
